compliance_matrix.name: Elite
compliance_matrix.label: Tier 1 — Elite
compliance_matrix.compliance_criteria: TLS 1.3 with ML-KEM key exchange + ML-DSA authentication + AES-256-GCM + SHA-384
compliance_matrix.priority_action: Maintain posture; monitor NIST PQC evolution; implement SLH-DSA backup.

compliance_matrix.name: Standard
compliance_matrix.label: Tier 2 — Standard
compliance_matrix.compliance_criteria: TLS 1.2/1.3 with at least one PQC algorithm (ML-KEM or ML-DSA) in use.
compliance_matrix.priority_action: Complete PQC migration. Prioritize remaining classical KX/auth components.

compliance_matrix.name: Needs Improvement
compliance_matrix.label: Tier 3 — Needs Improvement
compliance_matrix.compliance_criteria: TLS 1.2+ with AES-256 + SHA-256/384, but RSA/ECDSA still used for KX/auth.
compliance_matrix.priority_action: Initiate PQC migration project. Prioritize HNDL-exposed assets immediately.

compliance_matrix.name: Critical
compliance_matrix.label: Tier 4 — Critical
compliance_matrix.compliance_criteria: Legacy algorithms in use: RSA-1024, SHA-1, RC4, TLS 1.0/1.1, or DES.
compliance_matrix.priority_action: URGENT: Immediate remediation required. Disable legacy protocols. Apply PQC migration plan.

classification_table.description: All components fully quantum-safe per NIST PQC standards
Classical algorithms only, symmetric/hash are quantum-safe
Vulnerable legacy algorithms in active use
